executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 Canadian organizations compromised in the past 48 hours across all sectors, including high-profile targets like Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Kids & Company, and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware gang leads the assault with 20 victims, followed by safepay (13), play (12), and a massive Clop campaign (14 victims). Separately, two critical vulnerabilities affecting Linux systems (CVE-2026-31431) and cPanel/WHM (CVE-2026-41940) have been flagged by the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security requiring immediate patching. The scale and coordination of these ransomware attacks suggest a sustained, organized campaign targeting Canadian infrastructure.

items.cia_impact: Unknown - CCCS advisory requires review for details

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is under severe ransomware siege with 101 organizations compromised across every sector in a 48-hour period. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security has issued two high-priority alerts for CVE-2026-31431 (Linux) and CVE-2026-41940 (cPanel/WHM). Critical infrastructure targets include public health agencies, school boards, municipalities, telecommunications, and financial services, indicating coordinated targeting of Canadian entities.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by coinbasecartel ransomware gang; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) compromised by sinobi gang.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company database leaked by everest ransomware; Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by sinobi; Trucash compromised by lynx; multiple accounting and financial services firms affected including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, Axion50plus, and reidhurstnagy.com. No major banks (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) identified in breach reports.

report_2w.executive_summary: Cisco ASA and Firepower devices face critical vulnerabilities (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) now exploited in the wild via the FIRESTARTER backdoor, which survives patching and requires cold reboots to remove—CCCS issued an updated alert April 23. Medical device giant Medtronic confirmed a breach by ShinyHunters threatening data leak, while Vimeo and ADT also suffered third-party breaches exposing millions of user records. North Korean Lazarus/BlueNoroff groups are actively targeting cryptocurrency firms with AI-generated lures and ClickFix social engineering. Chinese national extradited to US over Silk Typhoon COVID research cyberattacks, and Scattered Spider member arrested in Finland pending extradition.

items.description: Cisco ASA and FTD vulnerability exploited by FIRESTARTER backdoor
items.software_affected: Cisco ASA and Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) devices
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Medium

items.description: Microsoft Defender RedSun zero-day granting full SYSTEM access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.name: FIRESTARTER (unknown attribution)
items.targets: Cisco Firepower devices globally
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1543 (Create or Modify System Process) - Linux backdoor surviving patches and requiring cold boot removal

items.name: Silk Typhoon (Chinese state-sponsored)
items.targets: COVID research institutions
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) - pandemic-era cyberattacks targeting research

items.name: Lazarus Group / BlueNoroff (North Korean APT)
items.targets: Cryptocurrency firms and developers
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1204 (User Execution), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter) - AI-generated Zoom lures, ClickFix social engineering, PromptMink supply chain attacks targeting crypto developers

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Medtronic, Vimeo, Udemy
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - multiple healthcare and tech breaches with ransom threats

items.name: Scattered Spider (UNC3944)
items.targets: Various enterprises
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Spearphishing Link), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) - social engineering and identity-based attacks

items.name: Sandworm (Russian GRU Unit 74455)
items.targets: Critical infrastructure
items.ttps: T1090.003 (Multi-hop Proxy), T1021.004 (SSH), T1572 (Protocol Tunneling) - SSH-over-Tor tunneling for stealthy long-term persistence

items.name: OilRig (APT34, Iranian)
items.targets: Middle East organizations
items.ttps: T1102 (Web Service), T1001.002 (Steganography), T1071.001 (Web Protocols) - hiding C2 configuration in Google Drive images using LSB steganography

items.name: Qilin (ransomware group)
items.targets: Exclusive Networks, Leone Film Group, Longwood Engineering, ANZ organizations
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - ransomware operations targeting IT distributors and enterprises

items.name: RansomHouse
items.targets: Barracuda Networks (claimed, unverified)
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - claimed breach of cybersecurity vendor

items.name: PhantomCore
items.targets: Russian networks via TrueConf
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1204.001 (Malicious Link), T1204.002 (Malicious File) - exploiting TrueConf vulnerabilities, ClickFix technique

items.name: Chinese-backed smishing rings
items.targets: Global credential theft via SMS and OTT apps
items.ttps: T1566.003 (Spearphishing via Service), T1056.003 (Web Portal Capture), T1598 (Phishing for Information) - large-scale SMS phishing operations

items.name: TeamPCP
items.targets: Checkmarx (supply chain attack)
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - supply chain attack leading to dark web data leak

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL25-012 on April 23, 2026, warning Canadian organizations about actively exploited vulnerabilities in Cisco ASA and FTD devices (CVE-2025-20333, CVE-2025-20362, CVE-2025-20363) being leveraged by the FIRESTARTER backdoor. Manitoba announced plans to ban social media and AI chatbots for youth. No other Canada-specific cyber incidents or breaches of major retailers or financial institutions were identified in this reporting period.
